content.mi:
Kua Waihotia

I Whakaari, ka oti i a Te Tahi ngā kawa.

I marara te iwi huri noa i te motu ki te kohi tītī, ā, ka whakatā te tohunga kaumātua i raro i te rā mahana.

Engari, i tana oho ake…

kāore he tangata.

Kāore he waka.

Kāore he reo.

Kāore ngā tāngata i whakawhirinaki ai ia.

Katoa kua ngaro.

Kua whakarerea a Te Tahi-o-Te-Rā.
content.en:
Left Alone

On Whakaari, Te Tahi completed the rituals.

The people scattered across the island to gather tītī, while the old tohunga rested in the warm sun.

But when he awoke…

everyone was gone.

The waka.

The voices.

The people he trusted.

All of them.

Te Tahi-o-Te-Rā had been abandoned.
